title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH's pediatric cancer care and research.
Mahr previously ran in 2014, inspired by young patients at Heartbreak Hill and the legacy of Rick and Dick Hoyt.
He emphasizes the need for funding pediatric cancer research, citing the challenges faced by children and families at MGH.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts plans to reduce the income tax rate from 5% to 4%, potentially cutting state revenue by $5.1 billion per year.
A Boston.com poll shows 78% support for the tax cut, with expected annual savings of $1,250 for the median household.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing risks to economic competitiveness and funding for state programs.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for first-degree murder, accused of poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze.
Prosecutors argue Church's jokes about poisoning Fowler reflect a genuine plot due to his affair and claim she poisoned his drink.
Defense alleges Church's comments were jokes out of context and highlights Fowler's substance abuse as a possible cause of illness.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposed lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and allowing hunters to use crossbows.
The proposed legislation would reduce bowhunting setback distances, opening over 40,000 acres for hunting.
MassWildlife and Mass Audubon support the changes, citing overpopulation of deer impacting local ecosystems.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol in a case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The discovery was shared in a YouTube video, gaining over 1,700 views and wide attention on social media.
DeMontigny reported the firearm to police and noted the historical significance of derringer pistols.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, faces murder charges after a fatal stabbing in Chelsea on Thursday.
Chelsea police responded to a stabbing at 6:45 p.m. near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, finding multiple victims.
A 25-year-old victim died from stab wounds during surgery; the suspect is to be arraigned in Chelsea District Court.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers were injured during a struggle with a knife-wielding suspect at Forest Hills MBTA station in Jamaica Plain.
The suspect attempted to stab bus tires and reached for an officer's weapon, leading to a gun discharge without injury.
The upper busway was closed during police activity, with regular service resuming by 1:30 p.m. after the incident.

title: Man accused of kicking dog in Roxbury before being assaulted by group
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man is charged with animal cruelty for allegedly kicking a dog into the street in Roxbury.
Five unidentified men allegedly assaulted the suspect shortly after he kicked the dog near 559 Dudley St.
The missing dog, named Biscuit, has not been found, and an alert was issued for its recovery.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cambridge resident Alden Welch Ruml, 26, charged with emailing a threat to FBI Director Kash Patel's girlfriend, Alexis Wilkins.
Ruml allegedly emailed after reading about FBI resources for Wilkins, a country singer, receiving security protection.
Ruml arrested on March 13, pleaded not guilty; faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: Board a lobster boat and dine on your catch while staying at this Boston hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Lenox hotel in Boston offers the Boston Harbor Lobster Experience Package starting in May 2026.
Guests can board a lobster boat for a tour, catch their meal, and enjoy a five-course dinner.
The package includes overnight stay, lobster dinner at Willow & Ivy, and all gratuities, costing $1,650 for two.
